channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Control Resonant Is Looking To ‘Attract The Action RPG Crowd’
item.content:encoded:
Control Resonant is taking some pretty big swings for a sequel, with numerous aspects from the original being heavily altered. Not only is there a new protagonist and setting, but Resonant is breaking into an entirely different genre, with guns being ditched for melee action instead.

It’s a bold step in a new direction, especially for a studio that’s practically mastered the art of the third-person shooter, but could it come with the risk of alienating the original fanbase? Remedy doesn’t seem to think so; instead, it’s seen as an opportunity to reach an entirely new audience—namely, the action RPG crowd.

Before going any further, consider downloading the free Insider Gaming app for the cleanest way to read your gaming news on iOS and Android.

Resonant Is All About Introducing More People To Control

Speaking with the Art Director for Control Resonant, Elmeri Raitanen, we discussed the risks and rewards of the bold shift from third-person shooter to melee-action brawler.

When exploring why Remedy chose to make this change, Raitanen settled on a handful of reasons.

Firstly, it made sense for the narrative. It was always the plan for the sequel to be Dylan’s story, and with numerous themes of juxtaposition and contrast between the Faden siblings, it was decided very early on that he would serve as the ‘brawler who gets up close and personal,’ compared to Jesse’s more ranged approach.

  • Related: Future Control Titles Depend On If Resonant ‘Does Well’

However, a new genre also meant an opportunity to reach a wider player base, and Remedy seems keen to do so. Raitanen explained that fans who might prefer action RPGs didn’t give Control a go, and that’s a ‘massive player base who might have enjoyed Control if it was a little more in their wheelhouse.’

With that in mind, there’s hope that Resonant could ‘attract a little more of that action RPG crowd,’ with more eyes on the game the better. That did prompt me to question whether there’s a risk that, with a new genre, could fans of the original potentially be alienated by this new experience? Raitanen was encouraging in his response:

We have been thinking about it, of course. I could still say that, regardless of the many things that we have changed, there is still a strong undercurrent of the original vibes of Control in this one as well.

It’s undoubtedly a huge shift, but having played the game for myself, I can verify that Resonant still feels like Control, and judging by the combat so far, you wouldn’t think this is Remedy’s first foray into the melee-action genre.

Still, it’s a topic worth discussing. How do you feel about Control Resonant shifting genres? Do you wish it remained a third-person shooter, or are you excited to give the melee action a go? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more gaming news, read about Destiny 2’s final update not moving the needle at Bungie, and don’t forget to subscribe to the Insider Gaming Newsletter.

Charlie is one of Insider Gaming's Guides Writers who's been in the gaming industry for a little over a year. Unfortunately for him, he enjoys every video game genre out…

More from Charlie Champion

item.title: All Forza Horizon 6 Horizon Decades Summer Festival Playlist Events, Challenges & Rewards
item.content:encoded:
A fresh week in Forza Horizon 6 means a new slew of content to churn through, and the rewards for the next month during Series 2, Horizon Decades, begin with a return to Summer.

Festival Playlists in Forza Horizon 6 are spread across four weeks, each featuring a different season with map changes and challenges, before moving on to the next playlist. Now, Horizon Decades kicks off with Summer.

Before going any further, download the free Insider Gaming App for the cleanest way to read your gaming news on iOS and Android.

All Horizon Decades Summer Festival Playlist Daily Challenges & Rewards

As usual, Daily Challenges in Forza Horizon 6 drop on a daily basis, but they remain available until the end of the current season, and potentially even longer.

This means that you don’t have to make sure you log into the game daily to tick off the objectives, and can instead stack them up to create a to-do list to tick off before the end of the season.

  • Related: Forza Horizon 6 Giving All Players a Free Car After Eliminator Issues

The seven Daily Challenges for the Summer season in the Horizon Decades Festival Playlist and the rewards they provide are outlined in the table below.

All Horizon Decades Summer Festival Playlist Weekly Challenges & Rewards

As ever, Weekly Challenges in Forza Horizon 6 are themed around a specific vehicle, as this week’s car of choice is the DeLorean DMC-12.

You’ll need to own the car to tick through the challenges, which must be completed in the set order, and the final reward is Five Festival Points and 25,000 Credits. The good news, however, is that you can earn the car by winning a Seasonal Championship.

Each step of the Weekly Challenge in the Forza Horizon 6 Horizon Decades Summer Festival Playlist is detailed below.

  1. Own and drive the DeLorean DMC-12.
  2. Maintain a speed of 88mph for 10 seconds.
  3. Take a photo of the DeLorean DMC-12.
  4. Win a Road Race in the DeLorean DMC-12

All Horizon Decades Summer Festival Playlist Events & Rewards

The table below highlights all other Seasonal events and challenges in the Welcome to Japan Spring Festival Playlist, and all the available rewards.

Check out the Horizon Decades Series trailer below.

What do you make of the rewards available this week? Share your thoughts in the Insider Gaming Discord.

For your next read, take a look at all the Wheelspin Exclusive cars in Forza Horizon 6, and don’t forget to sign up

MOBILE APP

item.title: Escape from Tarkov Roadmap Details Huge 1.1.0 Patch
item.content:encoded:
Battlestate Games has published an all-new roadmap for Escape from Tarkov, uncovering everything being added or changed in the stellar extraction shooter over the next few months. It includes an in-game event in July called Blackout, and then a massive 1.1.0 patch that introduces the game’s first season as part of a fresh, exciting system.

The team also revealed what’s coming beyond that monumental update, including a rework to Lighthouse, which has been teased in recent weeks, the touted GPS mechanic, and the deployment of Unity 6.

Before going any further, consider downloading the free Insider Gaming app for the cleanest way to read your gaming news on iOS and Android.

Tarkov Has Massive Changes Coming

In the recent post from Battlestate Games, it was revealed that the remainder of June will yield ‘regular mini events’, and then in July, an event called Blackout will hit the platform.

Also in July comes the 1.1.0 update, an enormous patch with stacks of content and changes:

  • Seasons system and Season 1 launch
  • Technical changes focused on game stability and performance
  • Reworked task system
  • Group shared kill objectives for tasks
  • Rebalanced insurance system
  • Rebalanced hideout progression
  • Improved beginner assistance packs
  • Streets of Tarkov optimization
  • AI behavior improvements and fixes
  • Expanded interactive mechanics on locations
  • New weapons and gear
  • New PMC customization options
  • Observer camera
  • Additional anti-cheat measures

If that wasn’t enough, Q3 2026 is set to bring about further amendments to the extraction ecosystem, including another in-game event and a much-needed rework of Lighthouse, arguably the most hated Escape from Tarkov map.

There will also be a skip Prestige requirements function added, but it’ll cost a ‘large in-game fee.’

  • RELATED: Get The Latest Escape from Tarkov Codes

New players will also be getting the simplified GPS mechanic that helps them navigate the game’s complex maps, and the extraction systems will be getting reworked.

Additionally, the trader system, loot pool, reputation system, and raid reconnect function will all be rebalanced or adjusted in some way.

For the tech-savvy folks out there, Q3 2026 will also bring about Unity 6 and FSR 4.0 for engine, graphical, and performance-based boosts.

Are you excited to see what comes this year for Escape from Tarkov? Let us know what you’re thinking on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that GTA 6’s cover art has been revealed, and be sure to subscribe to the Insider Gaming Newsletter.

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

MOBILE APP

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.title: “I don’t have pressure” – molodoy on FURIA form
item.content:encoded:
Insider Gaming spoke to Danil ‘molodoy’ Golubenko at the IEM Cologne 2026 Major to discuss FURIA’s emotions coming into the playoffs of the event.

The Brazilian organization won four events in 2025, but has yet to taste silverware in 2026. We discuss how FURIA aim to get to the top again, and FURIA’s potential path to the Grand Final at Cologne.

Before going any further, consider downloading the free Insider Gaming app for the cleanest way to read your gaming news on iOS and Android.

Insider Gaming interviews molodoy at the IEM Cologne 2026 Major

We’re here at the IEM Cologne Major playoffs. How are you feeling?

I’m good, I’m great, thank you.

Last year, you were riding high, you won four events in a row, you know, you had a great year. In 2026, it feels like it hasn’t really clicked as much. How would you contrast what’s happening in the game?

I think, first thing, the FalleN tihng and and this is, I would say, not good. But second thing, I think we are just trying to find our game.

Yeah, I think so.

Can you describe how you’re finding your game or what improvements you’ve made?

I’ll say I’m trying to change my game a little bit, and trying to help the team and trying to do more stuff. I think, yeah, that’s all.

So would that be more of a supportive role?

No, I’m not talking about support. I’m talking about all things, not only in-game, but before the game, what we do with this team and stuff like this.

  • Related: “We believe that he can be the difference-maker for us” – NiKo on playing with karrigan in the Lanxess

You guys had like two quite emotional events back to back because at IEM Rio and PGL Astana, do you have any reflections on those events?

I think in Rio and in Astana, it’s very good for us to come because it’s two, I’ll say it’s two home tournaments.

When you play this tournament, I think you’re taking a little bit of confidence in this tournament because people only support you, and I’ll say you take a little bit of motivation for yourself, and it’s very good for you and for us as well.

Do you feel like there have been moments where your performance has not been as good under that kind of pressure?

I don’t have pressure. I don’t have pressure. I’m just trying to do my best, and yeah, it’s all good.

So in terms of the team chemistry, we’ve seen yuurih not being able to find his timings, some rotations not working as well as last year. What are you working on to improve?

You know, maybe some habits, the call is not working. It’s always in Counter-Strike, sometimes maybe some gamble s**t’s working, and some stuff’s not working, and this is Counter-Strike.

It’s sometimes you can kill five guys, sometimes you can kill only one, you know, it’s Counter-Strike. Who knows what happens?

Your side of the bracket in the playoffs is maybe more favourable? How do you assess the potential matchups for you?

I cannot say it’s a kind of easy bracket. 9z plays well. We play against 9z in the first game. They beat very good teams in the PGL Astana. They beat good teams here too, and Aurora and BetBoom are good teams too. I cannot say it’s an easy bracket.

It’s a very good bracket. It’s always in a Major, you have a hard team.

How would you assess 9z’s strengths?

I’m not watching how they’re playing a lot of time, but I think I watch when they play against magic in PGL Astana. They’re playing good.

I like how they’re playing. A little bit gambly, but I like it.

In terms of, you know, your own individual form, do you feel like you’re online in the server?

I don’t know. We will see. We will see.

End of interview

Let us know your IEM Cologne 2026 Major predictions on Insider Gaming’s Discord


For other news, see the latest update on God of War Laufey and sign up to the Insider Gaming newsletter

Darragh is an Esports Journalist for Insider Gaming specialising in Counter-Strike. He loves to explore how esports teams work, or why they very often do not.

More from Darragh Harbinson

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/molodoy-iem-cologne-2026-major-interview/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: When Is GTA 6 Trailer 3 Releasing? (June 2026 Update)
item.content:encoded:
Grand Theft Auto 6 is going to take the world by storm this November, and the whole gaming industry has begun to prepare for its imminent arrival. Release dates have been chopped and changed, as everyone knows it’s fruitless to go up against the titan that will be GTA 6.

That said, there’s still one major question yet to be answered: when is the third trailer for GTA 6 releasing? It’s been over a year since we last saw Vice City in all of its glory, but as we edge nearer and nearer to its release date, it’s bound to release soon, right?

Here’s everything we know about when we can expect the third trailer for GTA 6. But, before you continue, consider downloading the free Insider Gaming app for the cleanest way to read your gaming news on iOS and Android.

When Is Trailer 3 For GTA 6 Releasing?

As things stand, there is no confirmed date for the next Grand Theft Auto 6 trailer. That said, we have every reason to suspect it could be on the horizon.

Rockstar has officially confirmed that GTA 6 pre-orders will go live on June 25, finally giving fans a date they’ve been waiting years for. Alongside the announcement, the studio also unveiled the game’s official box art, leaving gameplay footage as one of the last major pieces of the marketing puzzle.

  • More Insider Gaming: When Is GTA 6 Coming Out On PC? GTA 6 PC Release, Explained

Naturally, many fans believe a third trailer could accompany the launch of pre-orders. After all, there’d be no better way to build excitement and drive sales than by showing off more of the game just before pre-orders open.

That said, this is GTA 6 we’re talking about. Rockstar could launch pre-orders tomorrow, remain completely silent until release, and the game would still sell millions of copies. Nothing says a new trailer has to arrive alongside pre-orders.

Still, if Rockstar follows the traditional marketing playbook, Trailer 3 feels closer than ever. The most likely scenario would see it arrive on June 25, ending with the announcement that pre-orders are now live.

Should anything be confirmed before the reveal, you’ll find this article aptly updated.

What Can We Expect From GTA 6 Trailer 3?

It’s all well and good hoping for another glimpse at GTA 6, but what can we realistically expect from the third trailer?

Many fans will be hoping for a closer look at gameplay. Most of what we’ve seen so far has been incredibly cinematic, highlighting the stunning views of Vice City and its surroundings, but a more grounded view of the driving, gunplay and general gameplay would go down as a treat.

  • More Insider Gaming: Everything We Learned About May 2026 Take-Two Earnings Call For GTA 6

With Rockstar confirming that the plans are to ramp up the marketing this Summer, a third trailer and pre-orders could very well be the start of a dominant Summer from Rockstar. Time will tell, but it’s starting to look like we have every reason to get excited. In the meantime, take a trip down memory lane and revisit Trailer 2!

What do you want to see from GTA 6’s third trailer? Share your wildest hopes in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more Insider Gaming content, check out the following, and be sure to subscribe to our Newsletter.

  • How To Earn 2500 Free Units In Marvel Rivals—Summer Festival 2026
  • Black Ops 1 & 2 Ports—Release, Platforms, Multiplayer, File Sizes & Remaster Status

Charlie is one of Insider Gaming's Guides Writers who's been in the gaming industry for a little over a year. Unfortunately for him, he enjoys every video game genre out…

More from Charlie Champion

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Burger Motorsports to Pause Operations for GTA 6 Release Day
item.content:encoded:
Burger Motorsports will temporarily pause operations on November 19, 2026, citing employee scheduling conflicts tied to GTA 6’s launch.

The release of Grand Theft Auto 6 continues to be anticipated as a major cultural event. The game will launch on November 19, as Take-Two Interactive CEO Strauss Zelnick has confirmed several times. In anticipation of employees being too preoccupied to come to work, one business has already decided to temporarily pause operations that day.

The business in question is a burger joint (in name only), but before getting into the meat of the matter, here’s how you can have the cleanest reading experience for this and all of our stories: download the free Insider Gaming App for iOS or Android.  

Burger Motorsports Closes for GTA 6 Amid Staff Conflicts

Burger Motorsports is the business that made this decision. In an internal company memo shared on the brand’s social media pages, the company announced that it made this decision after reviewing multiple employee scheduling conflicts and realizing that everything might be tied to the game’s release

“Normal business operations are expected to resume once employees have completed their initial exploration, finished at least one mission, and returned to reality. We appreciate your patience and understanding during this unprecedented cultural event,” the closing of the memo reads.

  • More GTA 6 News: GTA 6 Pre-Order Hype Causes Huge Malware Concern

Rockstar Games recently announced that pre-orders for the game will be available starting June 25. This adds further confirmation that the release date is now firmly established. The game was initially scheduled for release in late 2025, then moved to May of this year, and has since been postponed to November.

How do you feel about this move by Burger Motorsports? Are you excited for the impending pre-orders for GTA 6? Sound off about this story and all others on our official Discord server.

For even more news on Grand Theft Auto from Insider Gaming, check out how much money GTA 5 is still bringing in. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.title: GTA 6 Official Boxart Revealed, Pre-Orders Go Live June 25
item.content:encoded:
Rockstar Games has officially revealed the box art for GTA 6 and announced that pre-orders will go live for the game on June 25.

The announcement was made on the official Rockstar Games social media accounts, where it was also revealed that players can now also download the game’s official cover art.

But before we get into the full story, consider downloading the free Insider Gaming app for the cleanest way to read your gaming news on iOS and Android.

GTA 6 Price and Pre-Orders

What hasn’t been revealed yet is how much GTA 6 will actually cost. Rumors have circulated for months that Rockstar Games and Take-Two Interactive could make GTA 6 the first-ever game to cost $100 for the standard edition.

Those rumors were reinforced by Take-Two Interactive’s repeated statements on investor calls that it will not commit to a price. But with now pre-orders set to go live for GTA 6 next week, we won’t have long to wait. But one thing is certain: it probably won’t cost 16p, as it did when the game was mistakenly listed on the PlayStation Store.

“Consumers pay for the value that you bring to them, and our job is to charge way way way less of the value delivery,” Zelnick said on GTA 6’s price.

You can watch the announcement of the cover art below:

As for when the much-anticipated GTA 6 trailer 3 will be revealed remains to be seen, but with pre-orders set to go live on June 25, it could suggest that Rockstar Games will have a new trailer followed by a call to action of the pre-order.

RELATED – GTA Actor Thinks Grand Theft Auto 6 ‘Warrants’ a $100 Price Tag

You can download the GTA 6 cover art here.

Will you be pre-ordering GTA 6? Let us know through our Insider Gaming Discord server.

Tom Henderson is Insider Gaming's Co-Founder and Editor-In-Chief. When he's not running one of the industry's leading independent video game websites, he's probably playing an FPS like Call of Duty,…

More from Tom Henderson
